title: paspalum scrobiculatum var. jardinii
intro: Paspalum scrobiculatum var. jardinii, also known as Kodo millet, is a type of small-grained millet that belongs to the grass family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, diabetes, and cardiovascular diseases. The health benefits of Paspalum scrobiculatum var. jardinii include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. In herbal preparations, Paspalum scrobiculatum var. jardinii is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or as a powdered supplement to support overall well-being.
